title: TPU v1 · MMMC and On-Chip Variation: Close Every Mode and PVT Corner
oneLine: Timing signoff cannot validate 700 MHz only at nominal conditions. Silicon spans SS-to-FF process variation, voltage fluctuates, temperature rises, modes change, and devices vary within one die. MMMC builds every mode × PVT/RC view, while OCV/AOCV/POCV derates paths. Setup is stressed at slow/low-voltage/hot conditions; hold at fast/high-voltage/cold. Every view must pass.

legend.name: Multiple modes
legend.desc: Functional, scan/test, and low-power modes activate different paths, clocks, and SDC constraints. Signing only functional operation leaves scan and low-power timing unverified.

legend.name: PVT corner matrix (P × V × T)
legend.desc: Process corners such as SS/TT/FF combine with voltage droop/overshoot and the expected temperature range. Each combination uses a matching characterized .lib. This matrix protects 700 MHz across real data-center conditions rather than nominal operation alone.

legend.name: Slow SS for setup / fast FF for hold
legend.desc: Slow process, low voltage, and high temperature maximize delay and setup risk. Fast process, high voltage, and low temperature minimize delay and expose hold failures. Both directions must pass; hold cannot be repaired by lowering frequency.

legend.name: On-chip variation (OCV/AOCV/POCV)
legend.desc: Local process variation, IR drop, and temperature gradients make cells on one die differ. OCV applies pessimistic launch/capture derates; AOCV uses stage count/distance and POCV uses statistical σ data to reduce excess pessimism while preserving margin, especially on long array paths.

legend.name: Joint worst-case signoff
legend.desc: MMMC evaluates mode × PVT/RC corner × variation together. PrimeTime-class scenarios require setup slack ≥0 under slow conditions and hold slack ≥0 under fast conditions. A single failing view fails overall timing signoff.

steps.cap: ① Enumerate functional, scan, and low-power modes with separate clocks, constraints, and active paths.
② Build the PVT matrix: SS/TT/FF process × nominal±X% voltage × low-to-high temperature, with a matching .lib for every corner.
③ Apply OCV/AOCV/POCV derates within each corner to cover same-die variation, with adequate margin on long array and accumulation paths.
④ Analyze joint mode × corner × derate views in PrimeTime-class MMMC; setup stresses SS/low-V/hot and hold stresses FF/high-V/cold.
⑤ Release only when setup and hold slack are nonnegative in every view; an omitted mode or corner remains an untested silicon condition.

before: List every mode and write its SDC: Include 700 MHz functional operation, scan shift/capture, and any low-power modes. Each mode needs its own clocks, I/O delays, exceptions, and case analysis.

Define PVT corners and libraries: Align with the foundry, package, and application environment on SS/TT/FF, nominal and ±X% voltage, and low-to-high junction temperature. Each view requires a corresponding characterized .lib.
Select the OCV method and data source: Choose OCV, AOCV, or POCV and document whether derates, tables, or statistical σ data come from foundry characterization or silicon calibration.
Prepare post-route SPEF for RC corners: Use real extracted parasitics for worst and best RC rather than wire estimates. Pair slow RC with setup analysis and fast RC with hold analysis.
Set criteria and waiver rules: Require illustrative setup/hold slack ≥0 with appropriate margin, and document every legitimate false, multicycle, or CDC exception by mode and corner.
during: Core rule: setup uses slow corners; hold uses fast corners. SS/low-V/hot exposes late arrival, while FF/high-V/cold exposes too-early arrival. Checking one direction cannot cover the other.
Analyze mode × corner views in parallel. PrimeTime-class tools combine mode, PVT, and RC into scenarios, reporting cross-combination violations that manual serial analysis can miss.

Apply variation margin without excessive pessimism. Flat OCV can overstate risk, especially on long array paths. AOCV by depth/distance or POCV by statistical data preserves safety while recovering justified margin.
Never treat frequency as a hold fix. Setup can improve with a longer period, but hold is period-independent and needs delay-buffer ECO at the fast corner.
Pair parasitics with the correct PVT corner. Slow process with worst RC supports setup; fast process with best RC supports hold. Incorrect pairing makes the matrix optimistic or meaningless.

State methodology provenance: MMMC, PVT corners, OCV/AOCV/POCV, PrimeTime scenarios, and corner-specific SPEF are standard signoff practices. TPU's corners, derates, mode constraints, and QoR are unpublished; ±X% and all criteria shown are illustrative.

result.label: Modes (example)
result.value: functional (700 MHz) / scan-shift / scan-capture / low-power, each with its own SDC

result.label: Voltage × temperature (example)
result.value: V = nominal ±X%; T = multiple low-to-high junction temperatures, each with .lib

result.label: On-chip variation
result.value: OCV/AOCV/POCV launch/capture derates; sufficient margin on long array links

result.label: Setup criterion (example)
result.value: All paths at SS/low-V/hot + derate have setup slack ≥0

result.label: Hold criterion (example)
result.value: All paths at FF/high-V/cold have hold slack ≥0; frequency cannot fix hold

after: Review WNS/TNS in every view: Confirm no mode×corner scenario retains negative slack, especially violations visible only in cross-combinations such as hot SS scan mode.
Return violations to ⑧ ECO and re-sign: Repair setup with faster cells, buffering, or clock adjustment; repair hold with delay cells. Rerun the same MMMC views and verify no other scenario regresses.

Reassess OCV/derates before chasing false failures: A cluster of tiny violations on long paths may indicate excessive flat-OCV pessimism. Move to AOCV/POCV only with characterized statistical support, never simply to force a pass.
Feed timing into the overall signoff gate: All-view MMMC/OCV timing is one dimension alongside EM/IR, DRC/LVS, LEC, and power. Tapeout requires every dimension green.
Archive the mode/corner manifest: Preserve modes, PVT/RC corners, OCV method and source, and per-view results so any field failure can be traced to whether that condition was signed.
source: In-Datacenter Performance Analysis of a TPU, Jouppi et al., ISCA 2017 (published TPU v1 array, 700 MHz/~92 TOPS, 24 MiB Unified Buffer, weight-stationary dataflow, 28 nm/≤331 mm²/28–40 W, and data-center deployment provide context. MMMC, PVT corners, OCV/AOCV/POCV, PrimeTime views, corner-specific SPEF, ±X%, and all criteria/margins are standard illustrative signoff practice, not private TPU reports, corners, or QoR.)
